title: S.C.A.L.E
fullTitle: Structural Causal Analysis of Labor & Education
domain: Causal Modeling | System Dynamics | Policy Evaluation | Computational Economics
description: Integrated computational policy-analysis framework studying long-term consequences of engineering education expansion in India. Combines System Dynamics, Structural Causal Models, and Agent-Based Modeling. Published in IEEE Access.
overview: S.C.A.L.E. is an integrated computational policy-analysis framework developed to study the long-term consequences of engineering education expansion in India. Rather than treating higher education, labor markets, and economic growth as independent systems, S.C.A.L.E. models them as a single dynamic ecosystem connected through delayed feedback loops, causal dependencies, and heterogeneous agent behavior. The framework combines System Dynamics (SD), Structural Causal Models (SCM), and Agent-Based Modeling (ABM) to evaluate how policy interventions influence graduate employability, underemployment, wage dynamics, migration, human-capital retention, and economic productivity over long time horizons.

features.label: Multi-Layer Causal Modeling
features.description: Integrated three paradigms: System Dynamics (macro), Structural Causal Modeling (causal inference), Agent-Based Modeling (individual decisions).

features.label: Long-Term Policy Simulation
features.description: Evaluates education policies over 15-year horizons accounting for delayed pipelines, labor-market adjustment, and nonlinear migration.

features.label: Counterfactual Policy Evaluation
features.description: Implements Pearl's do-calculus for policy regime simulation: Business-as-Usual, Seat Cap, Quality-First, Migration Friction, Optimal Mix.

features.label: Historical Calibration
features.description: Calibrated using longitudinal datasets (2010–2023): AICTE, AISHE, PLFS, RBI, CMIE, OECD, Ministry of Statistics.

features.label: Structural Causal Discovery
features.description: Estimated ATE = 0.4482: 1pp increase in enrollment share → ~44.8pp increase in graduate unemployment.

algorithms.name: System Dynamics
algorithms.description: Continuous stock-flow differential equations modeling enrollment, employment, GDP, wages.

algorithms.name: Structural Causal Model (SCM)
algorithms.description: DAG-based causal modeling with Pearl's do-operator for counterfactual interventions.

algorithms.name: Agent-Based Model (ABM)
algorithms.description: Individual graduate agents with heterogeneous decision rules: reservation wage, migration threshold, visa probability.

algorithms.name: Monte Carlo Simulation
algorithms.description: Stochastic simulation of policy scenarios with confidence intervals.

modules.name: System Dynamics Engine
modules.responsibility: Macro-level stock-flow equations for enrollment, employment, GDP, wages

modules.name: SCM Layer
modules.responsibility: Causal graph construction, do-calculus interventions, ATE estimation

modules.name: ABM Layer
modules.responsibility: Individual agent simulation with heterogeneous decision rules

modules.name: Calibration Module
modules.responsibility: Historical data fitting against AICTE, AISHE, PLFS, RBI datasets

modules.name: Policy Simulator
modules.responsibility: Five-regime policy evaluation with comparative metrics

results: Developed integrated SD–SCM–ABM framework for education policy evaluation
Demonstrated causal links between seat expansion and graduate employability
Optimal Integrated Policy Mix: 71.37% unemployment reduction, 1.31 GDP Index
Quality-First Reform consistently outperformed simple seat-cap regulation

publication.title: Unbalanced Expansion of Engineering Education in India: A Data-Driven Policy Analysis
